description: **PRQ BEGINNER STR** [gain STRENGTH]: Break rocks in the Queens Quarry into smaller, manageable stones.
onComplete: Standing in the dusty Quarry, where magical SHARDS can sometimes be unearthed using ``!work``, you feel a small surge of strength (STR + 0.001). As you catch your breath, a guard passes by and hands you a flyer for the Guild of Guards. Also. your noble might buy these 32 stones you've gathered.

description: **PRQ BEGINNER STL** [gain STEALTH]: Steal some stones without alerting the Quarry overseers.
onComplete: You sneak around the bustling Quarry, where the only light is from magical SHARDS which can sometimes be unearthed using ``!work``, you discover a hidden talent for stealth (STL + 0.001). Out of the shadows behind you, a thief slips a flyer for the **Guild of Thieves** into your pocket. Also. your noble might buy these 32 stones you've gathered.

description: **PRQ BEGINNER KNW** [gain KNOWLEDGE]: Identify valuable stones overlooked by others in the quarry.
onComplete: You scan the Quarry with your sharp eyes, and quickly learn that you need tools to mine the magical SHARDS using ``!work`` here. You grow a little wiser with every ``!look`` (KNW + 0.001). A strange punk nods at your knowingness and hands you a flyer for the **Guild of Spies**. Also. your noble might buy these 32 stones you've gathered.

description: **PRQ BEGINNER FRT** [gain FORTITUDE]: Identify valuable stones overlooked by others in the quarry.
onComplete: While others tire quickly, you can outlast them, patiently working through the endless debris and eventually learning that you need tools here to mine the magical SHARDS using ``!work``. This job suits you, and you feel resilient (FRT + 0.001). A robed rat somersaults before you and hands you a flyer for the **Guild of Monks**. Also. your noble might buy these 32 stones you've gathered.

description: **RSA**: Deliver supplies to Luna Base
onComplete: The delivery of supplies has helped the RSA establish a neutral outpost on Luna.

description: **RSA**: Deliver moon ores to Imperious Falls
onComplete: This delivery or ores will help the RSA build vessels for the expansion of the Underpunks in space.

description: **PRQ**: Deliver 100 oil to Luna Mesa Mart on Luna. [removes oil]
onComplete: You have SOLD 100 oil to the Luna Mesa Mart. Players can now purchase this from the LYCANOX faction at a marked up price.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: Use ``!tp`` to teleport to Jupiter Space Station (costing 50 coin). Once there report for work by using ``!job`` again.
onComplete: You have secured employment collecting trash from the Docking Port and taking it to the Storage Sector. Use `!job` again here [zone 55] to pick up trash. Use `!move s`  to get to the Storage Sector [zone 58]. Use `!job` again to dump it in the trash compactor.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Docking Port is cluttered with abandoned travel brochures and worn-out hydraulic hoses. Gather this portside rubbish and deliver it to the station's trash compactor. Use ``!move s`` to travel to the Storage Sector [zone 58]. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.01]
onComplete: You toss the greasy travel pamphlets and leaky hoses into the compactor. The thrill of hitting the eject button and watching them float away, never gets old! Use ``!move n`` to get more trash from the Docking Port.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Habitation Hub is littered with mismatched socks and ancient snack wrappers. Collect the clammy debris and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move s`` to Docking Port then ``!move s`` to Storage Sector. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.02]
onComplete: You dump the the moldy socks and unsavory snack wrappers into the compactor. Hitting the eject button is still satisfying! Use ``!move n`` twice to get back to the Docking Port.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Observation Deck is scattered with cracked telescope lenses and unreadable star charts. Collect this celestial junk and deliver it to the trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move w`` to Docking Port then ``!move s``to Storage Sector. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.03]
onComplete: You dump the fragmented lenses and useless charts into the compactor. Hitting the eject button and seeing them drift into space is astronomically satisfying! Use ``!move n`` then ``!move e`` to get back to the Observation Deck.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Medical Module is filled with used bandages and expired medications. Collect this medical waste and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move e`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.04]
onComplete: You dispose of the used bandages and expired meds in the compactor. Hitting the eject button and watching the medical waste float away feels oddly therapeutic! Use ``!move w`` to get back to the Medical Module.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Life Support System is cluttered with tangled vines and moldy air filters. Gather this organic junk and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move w`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.05]
onComplete: You collect the tangled vines and moldy air filters, tossing them into the compactor. Hitting the eject button and watching them drift into space is always followed by a joyous breath! Use ``!move e`` to get back to the Life Support System.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Research Ring is cluttered with expired experiment samples and broken test tubes. Gather this scientific refuse and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move w``, ``!move s``, ``!move s`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.06]
onComplete: You dispose of the expired samples and broken test tubes. Pressing the eject button and watching them fly into space is a nerdy thrill! Use ``!move n``, ``!move n``, ``!move e`` to get back to the Research Ring.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Engineering Bay is packed with burnt-out circuit boards and depleted energy packs. Collect this technical trash and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move e`` then ``!move s`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.07]
onComplete: You throw the Scorched circuit boards and drained energy cells in the compactor. The eject button tempts you, and launching the trash into space feels like a mini-engineering marvel! Use ``!move n`` then ``!move w`` to get back to the Engineering Bay.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: Gather the outdated directives and frivolous trash. Deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move w``, ``!move s``, ``!move s`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.08]
onComplete: You dump the trash in the empty trash compactor and still can't resist hitting the eject button. Watching it launch into space is always the best part. Wait!! Was that a rat??? Maybe you should teleport back to Ratropia and train some skills to unlock more station jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: Take this SCRAP you put aside and take it to your locker in the Docking bay. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.09]
onComplete: You secure your stash of scrap. Scrap is used to ``!repair`` metal frame spacecraft and vessels.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you practice your SINGING you'll get better work here. For now, you start serenading the space tourists here in the Observation Lounge and need to herd them towards their shuttle in the Docking Port. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.10]
onComplete: Your singing has driven all the space travelers into an urgency to leave. Work in the Queens Quarry to unlock more SINGING jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you skill to Strength 1 you'll get better work here. For now, try and get these drunken Revenants to stop breaking the furniture. You any good at arm wrestling? [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.11]
onComplete: Not bad! Laughing at your little ratty arms, every revenant wanted a turn at tossing you across the room. They've drunk less ale and passed out from exhaustion. Join the Guild of Guards to unlock more STRENGTH jobs!

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you skill to Stealth 1 you'll get better work here. For now, sneak these confidential documents to a couple of shady revenants waiting in the Docking Bay, without being seen. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.12]
onComplete: Nice work! You delivered those documents secretly and without incident. Join the Guild of Thieves to unlock more STEALTH jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you skill to Knowledge 1 you'll get better work here. For now, there's a dethpicable duck pooping on the hors d'oeuvres in the Observation Deck. It might like the leafy greens in the Life Support module better. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.13]
onComplete: You've relocated a 24th and a half century duck back to the Life Support module. Join the Guild of Spies to unlock more KNOWLEDGE jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you skill FORTITUDE you'll get better work here. For now, go to the Medical Module and volunteer for medical trials. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.14]
onComplete: You've proven your endurance as a formidable lab rat. Join the Guild of Monks to unlock more FORTITUDE jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you skill PANACHE you'll get better work here. For now, deliver these extravagant plants from the Life Support System to the Command Nexus. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.15]
onComplete: You have transformed the Command nexus by replacing the dead pot pot plant and impressed everyone. Busk to unlock more PANACHE jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you reach Leadership 1 you'll get better work here. For now you can find somewhere to put all these cats. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.16]
onComplete: Yep! This looks like as good a place as any and the Fak Cats seem to be right at home. Equip and command a follower to unlock more LEADERSHIP jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you skill to Medicine 1 you'll get better work here. For now, see if you can rustle up something to eat for the command staff! [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.17]
onComplete: You serve up a 5 course out of the only edible thing you could find. Then quickly retreat before they realise what they are eating. Purchase a FAK on Atomic Hub to unlock more MEDICINE jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you skill to Astro 1 you'll get better work here. For now, take these weird smelling cookies to the Observation Deck [zone 56] . Those Space Traffic Controllers might be getting hungry. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.18]
onComplete: The room is full of epic hulking mutanoids, each four times your size. While they are grateful for the snack, you realize you should have brought more cookies. They're still looking at you like you might be the main course. Rent a spacecraft from the Docking Bay for 500 coins to unlock more ASTRO jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you train MAGIC you'll get better work here. For now, transport an enchanted artifacts from the Research Ring to the safe in the Command Nexus. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.19]
onComplete: You've safely delivered the enchanted artifact. Learn to read to access the Magic Academy and unlock more MAGIC jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you skill CORRUPTION you'll get better work here. For now, deliver these dubious packages from the Docking Port to the Habitation Hub without raising suspicion. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.20]
onComplete: You've successfully delivered the now bleeding packages. Spend time in Barrows End to unlock more CORRUPTION jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: If you 'patreon' to Tech 1 you'll get better work here. For now, go poke something in the Engineering Bay. Its the best way to learn. [PRQ SQUIRE: REP 0.21]
onComplete: :zap: with a little help from your stupidity, you licked instead of poked and now the big board full of tiny lights marked 'research Ring' is flashing red. You quickly grab a supervisor and mime to him that you have detected (not caused) a crisis. Blurfgar! Murgblefaf! Hfroomph! Become a T5 patreon to unlock more TECH jobs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: Go to the Habitation Hub and announce the shift change with a yodel. [SNG 1]
onComplete: Your yodeling reverberates through the Habitation Hub, causing a couple of space ferrets to slo-mo swirl and hum Beethoven's Fifth, perfectly synchronised to end as Jupiter finishes its 10 hour spin. No one else notices, but they dawdle all the same.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Habitation Hub is choked with Revenant ale crates. Take them to the Storage Sector [zone 58]. If you're having trouble lifting them, just try not to break any. Revenant ale is not far from acid. [STR 1]
onComplete: You did it! You found a way to lighten the load without leaving evidence. However, you can't help but notice the lack of toilets on the emergency map. Should have brought the empties.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: Steal an arcane artifact and deliver it to Fran in the Docking Bay. [STL 1]
onComplete: Your covert plan involved sneaking the golden pan past Stan and successfully delivering it to Fran who was ecstatic and gave you a can of Jupiter Jam.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Life Support System in zone 59 is feeling under the weather. Your knowledge is needed to give it a boost. Start by checking the air filters. [KNW 1]
onComplete: Fantastic! Using your vast knowledge, you've pulled a duck out of the ductwork.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: Attention! Are you in need of some quick cash? Participate in our highly reputable medical trial at the Medical Module. No previous experience required, just a willingness to test the latest in interspecies skin transplants. [FRT 89]
onComplete: Congratulations! You've survived a questionable medical trial, far away from prying eyes. Your efforts have helped science (or something resembling it) take a step forward. Don't be alarmed if you sprout some odd hairs.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: You wake up after a dream where you're rich and decide the best way to earn money is to impress the station's elite with a performance at the Observation Deck. [PANACHE 1]
onComplete: Congratulations! Your performance was ok, more style over content, but ok. You've earned some coin, one rather clingy admirer, and a suspiciously enthusiastic offer to join the station's drama club.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: There's a leadership crisis, someone needs to herd a rowdy gang of Revenant Fak Cats out of the Medical Module [zone 57]. Your calm authority is needed to make this chaos someone else's problem. [LDR 1]
onComplete: Cattanooga! You've successfully transformed chaos into a well-oiled machine of well... Just slightly more hidden chaos! The fakkers can now fak without fak cats out fakking them

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The station's command staff are suffering from duck poisoning and need a dose of your medical expertise. Stat! [MED 1]
onComplete: Nice FAK! Your medical skills have revitalized the Command Nexus crew. Now they can make brilliant decisions like not eating Co2 charred duck.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: Navigate to Jupiter Station's Docking Port [zone 55]. Oh wait, you're already here. You haven't been eating Rev Cookies, have you? The Observation Deck [zone 56] needs an astro-savvy punk to monitor space traffic. Think you can handle the epic view of Jupiter and some semaphore flags? [AST 1]
onComplete: Well done! With your astro-navigation skills and the surprising lack of traffic, you've kept the docking lanes flowing smoothly. Who knew waving flags could be this exciting? 🕦🕥🕤🕜

description: **PRQ & PRV**: Due to the constant tug of war with Jupiter, the Command Nexus is in emotional turmoil. They need someone with psychic sensitivities to bring balance to chaos. Gather your thoughts here in the Research Ring, then proceed to the Command Nexus. [MGC 1]
onComplete: You arrive at the Command Nexus and immediately sense the swirling vortex of stress and anxiety. With a deep breath and a wave of your hand, you channel calm vibes and bring serenity to the room. Duration 5 mins.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Revenants have a 'special' delivery job that requires 'no questions asked'. Beware, engaging this close with the Revenants may give you an itch you can't scratch. [CRT 1]
onComplete: You hide the package inside the air vent manifold, feeling a strange immoral threshold has been crossed, you can't shake the feeling that something dark is taking root within you.

description: **PRQ & PRV**: The Research Ring's power supply is on the fritz. Take your tech-savvy skills to Engineering, find the right panel, and bring their power back online. Think of it as surgery, but with sparks. [TCH 1]
onComplete: Success! Thanks to your technical wizardry and a swift kick, the Research Lab is showing all green across the board. But now you have a strange urge for a cookie. Oh look, there one next to that passed out Revenant technician.

description: **PRQ**: Deliver a delicately balanced crate of Moon Dust Muffins back to Ratropia.
onComplete: It took a while for the lycanox to find a trade item that Ratropians liked. Who'd of thought zero-gravity baking was going to be such a hit with these ratty mini-me's. Especially the 'Stellar Strawberry' and 'Chocolate Crater Chip' ones, they're out of this world! You deliver the Moon Dust Muffins which have become a currency of camaraderie between Lycanox and Underpunks.

description: **PRQ**: Travel to the Oil Fields and converse with the F'aras (racers) to learn more about their sacred drug called Q'azraq (blue heart) to learn of its magical properties
onComplete: Your have an insightful conversation with a F'aras, yielding valuable information about the Q'azraq. This sacred substance could revolutionize our understanding and usage of mystical resources.

description: **PRQ**: Deliver a fragile crate of "Love Potion Number 28" to sell to the Lycanox eagerly congregating around the Luna Mesa Mart.
onComplete: You deliver the failed potion (#28) which is lethal to Underpunks, but in the hands of the Lycanox its a whirlwind of romance and inexplicable urges to compose bad poetry under the moons' naked glow. Recent whispers rumour that the potion not only enhances emotional connections but also, quite accidentally, the recipient's sense of rhythm - albeit with a whimsical proclivity for rhyming 'moon' with 'spoon.'

description: **PRQ**: Impress the local tavern owner with your performance, to secure a gig.
onComplete: Your performance captivates not only the tavern owner but also the eclectic mix of patrons at the Tavern. As the last note lingers in the air, applause erupts, securing you a regular spot on the stage.

description: **PRQ**: Teach a pack of dodgy sea rats at the rankle river docks to harmonise for a quirky street performance.
onComplete: Against all odds, and with the ever-present threat of a cutlass swinging for every off-note, you've successfully taught a motley crew of pirates and smugglers the finer points of harmony. As their gruff voices rise in somewhat melodious accord, not only do the local moss frogs flee in terror, but the crowd actually throws coin instead of the customary grenades and river weed. You've turned a few scallywags into bardy buccaneers, proving once again that music might just be mightier than the sword, or at least as mighty as a pirate's glare.

description: **PRQ**: Compose a jingle for a downshire blacksmith that makes his anvils sound appealing.
onComplete: With a creative spark as fiery as the blacksmith's forge, you've crafted a jingle that turns the clang of anvils into a call to arms for craftsmen far and wide. As this new tune catches on, not only do the blacksmith's sales improve, but locals now hum your melody too. Unfortunately they'll tire of it soon enough.

description: **PRQ**: Serve as a guest judge at a talent show in the Town Hall, where the talent is dubious, but the enthusiasm for cow bell solos is tragically genuine.
onComplete: With a discerning eye and an ear that's becoming all too familiar with cowbell solos, you've survived yet another performance at the Town Hall. Despite the dubious talent, your diplomatic applause and carefully chosen words managed to encourage the performer without inflating their ego. As you relish a brief respite, you can't help but hope that the next act might reveal a glimmer of genuine talent.

description: **PRQ**: Escort a famous performer to the Royal Tower through a sea of fans, armed only with your charm and a very persuasive tambourine.
onComplete: After successfully escorting the diva, you find yourself unexpectedly rubbing elbows with nobility and the Queen herself. Her Majesty seemed more intrigued with your hat, but after a brief chat, and an even briefer slice of surprisingly edible cake, you leave the palace with your pockets a bit heavier and soggier. Who knew that royal gigs were literal cake walks

description: **RTS**: Salvage an Uurt Tech Part from the battlefield around the "Juggling Fish Emporium" (asteroid #147645122, zone 0, oc 6, rc 79121) and ensure secure transport back to Kirsch 57's hanger (asteroid #168940424, zone 55, oc 12, rc 25415).
onComplete: After studying it, RTS will be able to sell the successfully transported enemy tech for Krypton. You have received your cut, but strained your relations with the Uurtians.

description: **Area 55**: Analyze and decode alien technology fragments
onComplete: Your analysis of the alien technology fragments have provided crucial insights, advancing RTS understanding and integration of extraterrestrial tech into Underpunk society.

description: **TFI**: Research Town Hall records for secrets surrounding the old banking vaults
onComplete: Your research surrounding the old Ratropia banking vaults have uncovered interesting clues of treasures and artifacts. These findings will greatly enrich the financial resources of Ratropia.

description: **TFI & ORT**: Use ``!tp`` to teleport to Oort Space Station (costing 50 coin). Once there report for work by using ``!job`` again.
onComplete: You have secured employment collecting trash from the Docking Port and taking it to the Storage Sector. Use `!job` again here [zone 55] to pick up trash. Use `!move s`  to get to the Storage Sector [zone 58]. Use `!job` again to dump it in the trash compactor.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Docking Port is cluttered with abandoned travel brochures and worn-out hydraulic hoses. Gather this portside rubbish and deliver it to the station's trash compactor. Use ``!move s`` to travel to the Storage Sector [zone 58]. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.01]
onComplete: You toss the greasy travel pamphlets and leaky hoses into the compactor. The thrill of hitting the eject button and watching them float away, never gets old! Use ``!move n`` to get more trash from the Docking Port.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Habitation Hub is littered with mismatched socks and ancient snack wrappers. Collect the clammy debris and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move s`` to Docking Port then ``!move s`` to Storage Sector. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.02]
onComplete: You dump the the moldy socks and unsavory snack wrappers into the compactor. Hitting the eject button is still satisfying! Use ``!move n`` twice to get back to the Docking Port.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Observation Deck is scattered with cracked telescope lenses and unreadable star charts. Collect this celestial junk and deliver it to the trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move w`` to Docking Port then ``!move s``to Storage Sector. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.03]
onComplete: You dump the fragmented lenses and useless charts into the compactor. Hitting the eject button and seeing them drift into space is astronomically satisfying! Use ``!move n`` then ``!move e`` to get back to the Observation Deck.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Medical Module is filled with used bandages and expired medications. Collect this medical waste and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move e`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.04]
onComplete: You dispose of the used bandages and expired meds in the compactor. Hitting the eject button and watching the medical waste float away feels oddly therapeutic! Use ``!move w`` to get back to the Medical Module.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Life Support System is cluttered with tangled vines and moldy air filters. Gather this organic junk and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move w`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.05]
onComplete: You collect the tangled vines and moldy air filters, tossing them into the compactor. Hitting the eject button and watching them drift into space is always followed by a joyous breath! Use ``!move e`` to get back to the Life Support System.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Research Ring is cluttered with expired experiment samples and broken test tubes. Gather this scientific refuse and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move w``, ``!move s``, ``!move s`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.06]
onComplete: You dispose of the expired samples and broken test tubes. Pressing the eject button and watching them fly into space is a nerdy thrill! Use ``!move n``, ``!move n``, ``!move e`` to get back to the Research Ring.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Engineering Bay is packed with burnt-out circuit boards and depleted energy packs. Collect this technical trash and deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move e`` then ``!move s`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.07]
onComplete: You throw the Scorched circuit boards and drained energy cells in the compactor. The eject button tempts you, and launching the trash into space feels like a mini-engineering marvel! Use ``!move n`` then ``!move w`` to get back to the Engineering Bay.

description: **TFI & ORT**: Gather the outdated directives and frivolous trash. Deliver it to the station's trash compactor in zone 58. Use ``!move w``, ``!move s``, ``!move s`` to travel to the Storage Sector. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.08]
onComplete: You dump the trash in the empty trash compactor and still can't resist hitting the eject button. Watching it launch into space is always the best part. Wait!! Was that a rat??? Maybe you should teleport back to Ratropia and train some skills to unlock more station jobs.

description: **TFI & ORT**: Take this SCRAP you put aside and take it to your locker in the Docking bay. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.09]

onComplete: Your singing has driven all the space travelers into an urgency to leave. Work in the Queens Quarry to unlock more SINGING jobs.

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you skill to Strength 1 you'll get better work here. For now, try and get these drunken Revenants to stop breaking the furniture. You any good at arm wrestling? [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.11]
onComplete: Not bad! Laughing at your little ratty arms, every revenant wanted a turn at tossing you across the room. They've drunk less ale and passed out from exhaustion. Join the Guild of Guards to unlock more STRENGTH jobs!

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you skill to Stealth 1 you'll get better work here. For now, sneak these confidential documents to a couple of shady revenants waiting in the Docking Bay, without being seen. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.12]
onComplete: Nice work! You delivered those documents secretly and without incident. Join the Guild of Thieves to unlock more STEALTH jobs.

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you skill to Knowledge 1 you'll get better work here. For now, there's a dethpicable duck pooping on the hors d'oeuvres in the Observation Deck. It might like the leafy greens in the Life Support module better. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.13]
onComplete: You've relocated a 24th and a half century duck back to the Life Support module. Join the Guild of Spies to unlock more KNOWLEDGE jobs.

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you skill FORTITUDE you'll get better work here. For now, go to the Medical Module and volunteer for medical trials. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.14]

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you reach Leadership 1 you'll get better work here. For now you can find somewhere to put all these cats. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.16]

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you skill to Astro 1 you'll get better work here. For now, take these weird smelling cookies to the Observation Deck [zone 56] . Those Space Traffic Controllers might be getting hungry. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.18]

They're still looking at you like you might be the main course. Rent a spacecraft from the Docking Bay for 500 coins to unlock more ASTRO jobs.

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you train MAGIC you'll get better work here. For now, transport an enchanted artifacts from the Research Ring to the safe in the Command Nexus. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.19]
onComplete: You've safely delivered the enchanted artifact. Learn to read to access the Magic Academy and unlock more MAGIC jobs.

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you skill CORRUPTION you'll get better work here. For now, deliver these dubious packages from the Docking Port to the Habitation Hub without raising suspicion. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.20]
onComplete: You've successfully delivered the now bleeding packages. Spend time in Barrows End to unlock more CORRUPTION jobs.

description: **TFI & ORT**: If you 'patreon' to Tech 1 you'll get better work here. For now, go poke something in the Engineering Bay. Its the best way to learn. [TFI SQUIRE: REP 0.21]
onComplete: :zap: with a little help from your stupidity, you licked instead of poked and now the big board full of tiny lights marked 'research Ring' is flashing red. You quickly grab a supervisor and mime to him that you have detected (not caused) a crisis. Blurfgar! Murgblefaf! Hfroomph! Become a T5 patreon to unlock more TECH jobs.

description: **TFI & ORT**: Go to the Habitation Hub and announce the shift change with a yodel. [SNG 1]
onComplete: Your yodeling reverberates through the Habitation Hub, perfectly synchronised to end as the OORT vs UURT war horns outside announce a short ceasefire for the solemn tradition of sock folding, a task both sides dutifully perform several times a day to maintain cosmic harmony in their lockers.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Habitation Hub is choked with Warp Wine crates. Take them to the Storage Sector [zone 58]. If you're having trouble lifting them, just try not to break any. Warp Wine can be highly explosive in zero gravity, but as long as the power stays on YOU'LL BE FINE. [STR 1]
onComplete: You did it! You found a clever way to lighten the crates without leaving evidence. However, you can't help but notice the lack of toilets on the emergency map. Maybe you should have brought the empty bottles.

description: **TFI & URT**: Steal some weapon plans, tag them with an encoded beacon, and eject them from the Trash Compactor in the Docking Bay. [STL 1]
onComplete: Your covert plan to profit from this war, regardless of who wins. Has resulted in a tiny sum from the enemy.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Life Support System in zone 59 is having a bit of an existential crisis. Your expertise is required to set reality straight. Begin by inspecting those finicky air filters. [KNW 1]
onComplete: Brilliant! With your encyclopedic knowledge, you've managed to extract a perplexed Black Sea jellyfish from the air filters. The Life Support System seems to be running smooth again, and the jellyfish has found a new home in the station's zero-gravity aquarium.

description: **TFI & ORT**: Brave Souls Needed! Need some quick cash? Sign up for our latest trial at the Experimental Cryo Tube in zone 57. You'll be testing a new gas-infused battle armor. No experience needed, just a willingness to try out experimental and ridiculously dangerous technology. [FRT 89]
onComplete: Congratulations! You've survived the perilous trial and lived to tell the tale. Your courage will advanced the Oortian War Machine. Don't be surprised if you find your a little taller (possible smaller) in the morning.

description: **TFI & ORT**: You wake up after dreaming of glory and decide the best way to earn your stripes is to entertain the battle-weary troops at the Observation Deck. [PANACHE 1]
onComplete: Congratulations! Your performance was shockingly outrageous, leaving some troops in stitches and others in stunned silence. You've earned some coin, an amorous one-eyed admirer, and a suspiciously enthusiastic offer to join the front lines for the Oortian cause. Don't be surprised if your next gig involves a lot more action.

description: **TFI & ORT**: Command calling! There's a small crisis brewing. We need someone to escort a horde of mischievous Frost Snorters out of the Medical Module in zone 57. Your calm leadership is required to herd them and their flatulence out of the Medical Module before someone gets a headache. [LDR 1]
onComplete: Ba ba ba... Brerrrfft!! You've expertly chased out the furry snort balls. Making their cloud of offensively malodorous gas someone else's problem. You've earned a reputation as the go-to leader for all things foul-smelling.

description: **TFI & ORT**: Emergency alert! The station's command staff have accidentally inhaled Frost Snorter gas and need your medical expertise. Stat! [MED 1]
onComplete: Nice FAK! Your medical promise has saved the Command Nexus crew from a noxious but intoxicating predicament.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Celestial War Observatory [zone 56] needs a space-savvy rat to monitor interstellar traffic. Think you can juggle the surreal view of the cosmos and our gas-powered communication balloons, all while war tourists watch? [AST 1]
onComplete: Well done! With your astro-navigation skills and the short lull in war traffic, you've kept the supply lanes flowing smoothly. Who knew a balloon popping job could be so exhilarating?

description: **TFI & ORT**: The relentless 1 million-year space battle has the Command Nexus on the brink of a exhaustion. They need someone with psychic sensitivities to bring a bit of hope to the despair. Start by gathering your thoughts here in the Research Ring, then proceed to the Command Nexus. [MGC 1]
onComplete: You arrive at the Command Nexus and immediately sense the overwhelming pessimism. With a deep breath and a wave of your hand, you channel calm vibes and infuse the room with a renewed sense of ambition. Duration: 5 minutes.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Uurtians have a 'special' delivery job that requires 'no questions asked'. Beware, engaging this close with the Uurtians may leave you feeling conflicted. [CRT 1]
onComplete: You hide the malodorous squeaking package inside the air vent manifold, feeling a strange immoral threshold has been crossed, you can't shake the feeling that something dark is taking root within you.

description: **TFI & ORT**: The Research Ring's power supply is kaput. Use your tech-savvy skills to head over to Engineering, find the correct panel, and bring their power back online. Think of it as surgery, but with sparks. [TCH 1]
onComplete: WHACK! Thanks to your technical wizardry and a swift kick, the Research Lab is showing all green across the board. But now you have a strange urge for a Warp Wine. Oh look, there's some next to that passed-out Oortian technician.

No one else notices, but they 'Hallelujah' all the same.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: The Habitation Hub is choked with crates of Courvoisier V.S. Take them to the Storage Sector [zone 58]. If you're having trouble lifting them, just try not to break any. Courvoisier V.S is now the rarest drink in the solar system. [STR 1]
onComplete: Chapeau! You've managed to lighten the load without leaving any evidence. However, you can't help but notice the lack of toilets on the emergency map. Should have brought the empties.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: Sneak past the often sleepy Pollux and deliver a secret message to Margote in the Docking Bay. [STL 1]
onComplete: You slipped past the Docking Bay guard and delivered the secret message to Margote, who was delighted and oddly gave you a magical flower. She leaned in and whispered, 'Tournicoti Tournicoton!' before disappearing into the shadows.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: The Life Support System in zone 59 is feeling drained. Your knowledge is needed to give it a boost. Start by checking the power conduits. [KNW 1]
onComplete: Mon Dieu! To your surprise, you've pried a bat-like drone off the battery array. You think about reporting this, but ouf, the paperwork!

description: **Attention!** Are you in need of some quick cash? Participate in our highly reputable experiment at the Medical Module. No previous experience required, just a willingness to test the latest in zero-atmosphere respiratory genetics. [FRT 89]
onComplete: Félicitations! You've survived a questionable experiment, far away from prying eyes. Your efforts have helped the 55th Legion to understand how the Lycanox are able to breathe in space. Don't be alarmed if you feel a sudden urge to howl at the moon.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: You wake up after a dream where you're rich and decide the best way to earn money is to impress the station's elite with an over-ambitious solo puppetry performance of 'Les Misérables' at the Observation Deck. [PANACHE 1]
onComplete: Bravo! Your performance was cut short, as the audience paid you to end early. You barely got past 'Look down, look down, you're here until you die.' Nonetheless, you've earned some coin and the respect of those who appreciate brevity.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: There's a leadership crisis, someone needs to herd a manifestation of Gobbals out of the Medical Module [zone 57]. Your calm authority is needed to make this menace someone else's problem. [LDR 1]
onComplete: Turns out it was just an illusion! There must be Shampunks onboard. Any actual reference to Gobbals being part of the Underpunks universe is nonsense and should not be construed as an infringement on any intellectual property. We would never do that, no matter how much we like Gobbals.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: The station's command staff are all lovelorn after falling in love with a rose. Get over there and see what this madness is about! [MED 1]
onComplete: You kinda helped... Turns out the command staff all ate Boa Constrictor for lunch. Now just the commander believes they are a snake.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: The Observation Deck [zone 56] needs an astro-savvy punk to monitor space traffic. Think you can handle the depressing view of Earth and some medieval messenger pigeons? [AST 1]
onComplete: Well done! With your astro-navigation skills and the surprising lack of traffic, you've kept the docking lanes flowing smoothly. Somehow you can't get the idea of a medieval messenger pigeon in a jet-powered space suit out of your head as you reach for your ImaginoMatic 5000. #fan-art post compulsary if you haven't already.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: Due to the constant push and pull of Earth's shifting gravitational anomalies, the Command Nexus is under extreme stress. This job needs someone with psychic sensitivities to remind them of their childhood. Gather your thoughts here in the Research Ring, then proceed to the Command Nexus. [MGC 1]
onComplete: You arrive at the Command Nexus and immediately sense the tumultuous stress caused by the need for constant adjustments to counter Earth's shifting gravitational anomalies. With a deep breath and a wave of your hand, you channel calm vibes and bring serenity to the room. [DURATION 5 minutes]

description: **HEX & 55TH**: A clandestine opportunity has arisen. A bourgeois Martian has a package that requires your discretion. Be wary, betraying the 55th in favor of the Martians may have unforeseen consequences. [CRT 1]
onComplete: You pick up the package from the Docking Port, its contents occasionally screeching and flapping. As you hide it in the Life Support System, you can't shake the feeling that something incurable is taking root within you. The Martian Bourgeois have made a small leap for mankind, and your betrayal is now complete.

description: **HEX & 55TH**: The Research Ring's power supply is 'foutu' due to Earth's gravitational anomalies. Utilize your tech-savvy skills to head to Engineering, locate the correct panel, and restore their power. Think of it as knitting a sweater, but with live wires. [TCH 1]
onComplete: Success! Thanks to your technical expertise and precise adjustments, the Research Lab is showing all systems green. But now you have a peculiar craving for a cognac. Oh look, there's a bottle next to that unconscious 55th technician.

description: **HEX**: Stealthily clear new paths above Ratropia and transport the rubble to the Quarry
onComplete: Your careful clearing of new paths above Ratropia and successful transportation of the rubble to the Quarry have made significant progress towards reaching the ancient laboratories.

description: **ZCP**: Venture to the tavern and gather tales of victory and superstition to unravel the secrets of luck
onComplete: You impressed the tavern regulars with your singing, gained their trust, and collected captivating tales and secrets from the luckiest adventurers.

description: **PRL & MRT**: The Habitation Hub is choked with crates of Martian Azure Absinthe. Take them to the Storage Sector [zone 58]. If you're having trouble lifting them, just try not to break any. Each bottle of Martian Azure Absinthe is a work of art. [STR 1]

description: **PRL & MRT**: Sneak past the often distracted Martian enforcers and deliver a secret message to Tannhäuser in the Docking Bay. [STL 1]

description: PRL & MRT: The Life Support System in zone 59 is malfunctioning. Your expertise is needed to stabilize it. Start by inspecting the quantum regulators. [KNW 1]
onComplete: To your astonishment, you discover a mouse-sized Martian drone tangled in the quantum regulators. It buzzes with a mechanical hum, its tiny metallic limbs twitching. Reporting it crosses your mind, but the thought of the paperwork quickly dissuades you. Like a faulty airlock seal, this odd pest keeps turning up.

description: Attention! Are you in need of some quick cash? Participate in our highly reputable experiment at the Medical Module. No previous experience required, just a willingness to explore the latest advancements in Martian bioengineering. [FRT 89]
onComplete: Congratulations! You've survived a subatomic experiment, far from prying eyes. Your participation has provided invaluable data to the Martian elite. As you leave, a strange sensation creeps over you, subtle changes that feel both fascinating and unsettling. You wonder what they might have done to you in the name of progress.

description: **PRL & MRT**: You wake up from a dream where you've just pulled off the Martian Train Robbery and resolve yourself to a safer way to make coin. Dazzle the station's elite with a one-person show at the Observation Deck. Your chosen act? Sam Spade's monologue from 'The Maltese Falcon.' [PANACHE 1]
onComplete: The applause started thin, but at the point when you tipped your fedora, their wallets opened. They paid you to wrap it up early. So you jump straight to... 'When a man's partner is killed, he's supposed to do something about it. It doesn't make any difference what you thought of him. He was your partner and you're supposed to do something about it.' As you pocket the cash, you notice the Martian elite nodding in subtle appreciation. Even in their world of polished steel and neon lights, brevity and audacity still have their place.

description: **PRL & MRT**: There's a leadership crisis, someone needs to herd a rebellion of robotic mice out of the Medical Module [zone 57]. Your calm authority is needed to make this menace someone else's problem. [LDR 1]
onComplete: After a tense standoff with the mechanical horde, you manage to drive the micebots out of the Medical Module. As the last one scurries away, it surprisingly Orwell's '1984': 'If you want a picture of the future, imagine a boot stamping on a human face - forever.' The haunting memory of those metallic creatures lingers.

description: **PRL & MRT**: The station's command staff are all measuring out thier lives with coffee spoons. Get over there and see what this addiction is all about! [MED 1]
onComplete: You found the command staff jittery and obsessed, brewing and savoring the new Martian coffee blend. After sneaking yourself a cup, you understand its allure-rich, dark, and mysteriously invigorating. The commander winks and throws you some coin before standing tall and quoting, 'With a bald spot in the middle of my hair - They will say: “How his hair is growing thin!” My morning coat, my collar mounting firmly to the chin, My necktie rich and modest, but asserted by a simple pin - They will say: “But how his arms and legs are thin!”' You slow walk out backwards, unnoticed.

description: **PRL & MRT**: The Observation Deck [zone 56] needs an astro-savvy punk to monitor space traffic. Think you can handle the view of tiered Martian space lanes over Mars and some high-tech navigation systems like the Quantum Flux Beacon Array and the HyperSpatial Vector Modulator? [AST 1]
onComplete: Well done! With your astro-savvy skills, you've kept the docking lanes flowing smoothly. The Quantum Flux Beacon Array and HyperSpatial Vector Modulator hummed with overkill precision, making the task feel absurdly simple. It was as if you were using a sledgehammer to swat a fly, but without all the heaving and sweating. All while making a decent cup of tea. #fan-art post compulsory if you haven't already.

description: **PRL**: The Command Nexus needs a Ratropian assistant. Your unique magical abilities make you the perfect candidate for this job. [MAGIC 1]
onComplete: ITS A TRAP!!! The Martians, coveting your magical abilities, attempt to capture you. Drawing on your psychic powers, you sense their intentions and manipulate their emotions, flooding them with fear and confusion. They scatter and panic, overwhelmed by the sudden emotional barrage, coins spilling from their pockets. You grab some compensation and manage to slip away unnoticed.

description: **PRL & MRT**: The Command Nexus has requested your presence in the Docking Port (Airlock 5) for a final, highly sensitive mission. Your reputation for handling critical tasks has not gone unnoticed. [CRT 1]
onComplete: As you approach the assembly of Martian officers, the pieces start to fall into place. The Neutrino Pulse Emitter, the Martian Azure Absinthe, the secret message to Tannhäuser, the malfunctioning Life Support System, the dubious experiment, the robotic mice, the coffee addiction, the Quantum Flux Beacon Array, and the trap set for your magical abilities - all interconnected in a web of deceit. The Martians were testing you, pushing your limits, and gathering data on your every move. Suddenly, the Air Lock doors slam shut behind you, and a voice echoes through the chamber, “Well done, Underpunk. You've proven invaluable to our research.” There is a bright FLASH! and your memory is wiped.

description: **PRL & MRT**: The Research Ring's power supply has been sabotaged. Head to Engineering, locate the correct panel, and restore power while keeping an eye out for anything unusual. [TCH 1]
onComplete: Success! Thanks to your technical expertise and precise adjustments, the Research Lab is showing all systems green. As you finish, you notice strange symbols etched into the panel and an unconscious Martian technician sprawled nearby. A peculiar craving for a Martian delicacy hits you, and you find a bottle with coins carefully laid out beside it. You take a swig and pocket the coins, but a nagging suspicion lingers - what did they really put in that bottle, and what have you really fixed?

description: **MSC & QNX**: The Command Nexus has requested your presence in the Docking Port (Airlock 5) for a final, highly sensitive mission. Your reputation for handling critical tasks has not gone unnoticed. [CRT 1]

The oil, the Toads, the secret message to Tannhäuser, the malfunctioning Life Support System, the dubious experiment, the Quantum Flux Beacon Array, and the trap set for your magical abilities - all interconnected in a web of deceit. The QNX were testing you, pushing your limits, and gathering data on your every move. Suddenly, the Air Lock doors slam shut behind you, and a voice echoes through the chamber, “Well done, Underpunk. You've proven invaluable to our research.” There is a bright FLASH! and your memory is wiped.

The human is laying in the corner, muttering "the copper sings in binary snores". A stack of coins tumbles from the robots chassis, glinting with molten edges - you snag them as the bot whirs, "PAYMENT FOR THE FLESH UNIT," before grinding back into the dark.
